message:
Default close_reason to "done" when no reason specified

Previously, when closing an issue without providing a --reason flag,
the close_reason field was set to None. This left the audit trail
incomplete and made it harder to distinguish between:
- Issues closed deliberately without comment
- Issues closed where the user forgot to provide context

This change defaults the close_reason to "done" when no explicit
reason is provided, ensuring every closed issue has at least a
minimal completion indicator in its audit trail.

Change details:

BEFORE:
  close_reason: args.reason.clone().map(Some),

  This meant:
  - If --reason="fixed bug" provided: close_reason = Some("fixed bug")
  - If --reason not provided: close_reason = None

AFTER:
  close_reason: Some(Some(args.reason.clone().unwrap_or_else(|| "done".to_string()))),

  This means:
  - If --reason="fixed bug" provided: close_reason = Some("fixed bug")
  - If --reason not provided: close_reason = Some("done")

Rationale:
- Consistent with common workflow patterns where "done" is implicit
- Improves JSONL export completeness for issue archaeology
- Aligns with bd (Go beads) behavior where closed issues have reasons
- Makes audit trail more informative for project statistics

Example usage remains unchanged:
  br close bd-abc123                    # Now sets reason to "done"
  br close bd-abc123 --reason "Fixed"   # Sets reason to "Fixed"

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
